page: Microsoft Azure FX64-16ms_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmsv2 family, designed for memory-intensive workloads. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz, offering 8 physical cores and 16 vCPUs via hyperthreading. A key hardware highlight is its massive memory capacity of 1344.0 GB, which translates to a resource density of 84.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The processor includes a large 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX (Advanced Matrix Extensions) for deep learning and mathematical operations.

Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core capabilities, with Geekbench single-core and text processing scores placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory performance is robust, with large-block write bandwidth reaching 91.25 GB/sec, categorized in the top 25%. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks also show strong performance, with gzip compression at 34.83 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.28 GB/sec. Multi-core operations, database transactions, and Redis SET operations perform in the average range, reflecting the lower core count relative to the massive memory pool.

This server does not include local storage, meaning it relies entirely on remote storage solutions, though it is designated as Premium Storage capable. It also provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The primary tradeoff of the FX64-16ms_v2 is its highly asymmetric resource allocation: it pairs a modest 16 vCPUs with an enormous memory footprint. This configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that require massive in-memory datasets but do not scale linearly with high CPU core counts, preventing organizations from paying for unneeded virtual cores just to acquire sufficient RAM.

The Microsoft Azure FX64-16ms_v2 is optimized for large-scale in-memory databases, heavy caching layers, enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems, and memory-bound data analytics. Its strong single-core performance and advanced matrix extensions also make it suitable for specific low-latency text processing, single-threaded serialization tasks, and lightweight machine learning inference workloads that fit entirely within its vast system memory.
description: The Microsoft Azure FX64-16ms_v2 is a highly memory-optimized server from the FXmsv2 family, built on the x86_64 architecture. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz with 8 physical cores and 16 vCPUs. The defining characteristic of this instance is its massive 1344.0 GB of RAM, yielding an unusually high 84.0 GB of memory per vCPU. While it lacks local storage and complimentary public IPv4 addresses, it is Premium Storage capable. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core and text processing performance, alongside strong large-block write memory bandwidth and single-threaded compression speeds. This server offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads by avoiding the need to provision excess vCPUs to obtain high memory capacity. It is best suited for large in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX64-16ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, 16 vCPUs, and an extraordinary 1344 GB of RAM, ideal for massive in-memory database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX64-16ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 16 vCPUs, 1344 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: An ultra-high-density memory instance pairing a high-frequency Intel Xeon processor with over one terabyte of RAM for memory-bound enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz
Massive 1344.0 GB of RAM providing 84.0 GB per vCPU
Top-tier single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
No local storage, designed for Premium Storage integration
Optimized for large-scale in-memory databases and enterprise caching
